title: AI with Python
tagline: Connect to GPT, build chatbots, and add intelligence to your code.

lessons.title: Make Your First AI API Call

accomplishments: Build a working chatbot in Python
Create an AI assistant that answers questions

do.title: Set up OpenAI and make your first AI call
steps: Install OpenAI library: pip install openai
Run it — you just talked to an AI!
do.code:
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI()  # Uses OPENAI_API_KEY env var

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-3.5-turbo",
    messages=[
        {"role": "user", "content": "Say hello and introduce yourself in one sentence."}
    ]
)

ai_message = response.choices[0].message.content
print("AI Response:", ai_message)
print("\nYour first AI API call worked!")
do.result: You just talked to an AI! The model read your message and generated a unique response.
do.proofRequired: your terminal showing a successful AI response

concepts.name: Large Language Models (LLMs)
concepts.definition: AI models trained on massive text to understand and generate human-like responses. GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, Llama are all LLMs.
concepts.analogy: A student who has read every book in every library. They can discuss any topic because they've seen so much text.

concepts.name: Prompts
concepts.definition: The instructions you give the AI. Better prompts = better responses. Types: direct questions, instructions, role-based ('You are a tutor'), few-shot (examples).
concepts.analogy: Ordering at a restaurant. 'Give me food' gets you something random. 'I want a medium-rare ribeye with mashed potatoes' gets exactly what you want.

concepts.name: Tokens
concepts.definition: Pieces of text the AI reads and generates. ~4 characters = 1 token. 1000 tokens ≈ 750 words. You pay per token.
concepts.analogy: Like phone minutes. Each word costs a tiny amount. More words = higher bill.

concepts.name: Chat Message Format
concepts.definition: Messages are a list of {role, content} objects. Roles: 'system' (sets behavior), 'user' (your messages), 'assistant' (AI responses). History enables conversation memory.
concepts.analogy: A script for a play. Each line has a character (role) and their dialogue (content).

concepts.name: Temperature
concepts.definition: Controls randomness (0.0-2.0). Low (0.1) = focused, deterministic. High (1.5) = creative, varied. Default 0.7 is a good balance.
concepts.analogy: A creativity dial. Turn it down for math answers, turn it up for poetry.

concepts.name: System Prompts
concepts.definition: The 'system' role message that shapes AI personality and behavior. 'You are a Python tutor' vs 'You are a pirate' produces completely different responses.
concepts.analogy: Giving an actor their character description before the scene. Same script, different performance.

understand.keyTakeaway: AI APIs let you add intelligence to any application. System prompts control behavior, messages enable conversation, tokens determine cost.

build.goal: Build a chatbot with conversation memory and custom personality

codeComparison.new:
messages = [
    {"role": "system", "content": "You are a friendly Python tutor."}
]

while True:
    user_input = input("You: ")
    if user_input == 'quit': break
    messages.append({"role": "user", "content": user_input})
    
    response = client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-3.5-turbo",
        messages=messages
    )
    ai_reply = response.choices[0].message.content
    messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": ai_reply})
    print(f"AI: {ai_reply}")

build.hint: The key to conversation memory: keep ALL messages in the list. Each new message adds to the history.

challenge.title: Build an AI-Powered Study Assistant
challenge.mission: Create a smart study tool that explains topics, generates quizzes, and grades answers.
requirements: Explain any topic in simple terms
Quiz the user and grade their answers
Save conversation history to JSON file
Track token usage and estimated costs
challenge.bonusChallenge: Add flashcard generation, difficulty levels, and learning progress tracking.

capabilities: Control AI behavior with system prompts

bridge.preview: Next: Build AI-Powered Tools — summarizers, translators, content generators, and more.

lessons.devFession: I spent $47 on OpenAI tokens in one day because I forgot to set max_tokens and my loop kept running. Always set limits. Always check your usage dashboard.
